name: Weyl-Heisenberg orbit generation for the d=12 branch (T-arm)

phase_0.domain_name: Weyl-Heisenberg orbit generation for the d=12 branch (T-arm)
phase_0.boundary_condition: TANCH: the complete SIC-POVM lattice closure

VINIT.justification: initial state before Weyl-Heisenberg action begins

TANCH.justification: terminal boundary containing the entire 144-element orbit

AFWD.justification: directed transformation mapping fiducial to orbit elements

AREV.justification: reverse transformation from measured probability back to fiducial proximity

IMSCRIB.justification: self-reference identity when fiducial measures itself

FSPLIT.justification: splits into 144 distinct WH orbits based on 12 primitive valuations

FFUSE.justification: reconstitutes full measurement space from individual orbits

EVALT.justification: confirmation that WH orbit generates full SIC structure

EVALF.justification: identification when orbit doesn't achieve desired SIC geometry

ENGAGR.justification: simultaneous true/false state where critical self-modeling occurs

IFIX.justification: irreversible storage of the exact d=12 fiducial vector

phase_3.true_description: WH orbit successfully generates SIC elements with |⟨ψᵢ|ψⱼ⟩|² = 1/13
phase_3.false_description: orbit fails to produce required equiangularity condition
phase_3.both_description: critical point where both successful and failed measurements coexist in paraconsistent framework

steps.domain_action: preparation of initial fiducial vector |ψ₀⟩ in 12-dimensional complex space
verification that frame potential F = 143/169 is achieved
branching into T-arm (Dimensionality primitives with 3 values each)
application of Weyl-Heisenberg displacement operators D(p,q) for p,q ∈ {0,...,11}
self-measurement ⟨ψ₀|D(p,q)|ψ₀⟩ for each displacement generating orbit elements
composition of displacement sequences to form complete 144-element orbit
identity recognition when displacement returns to fiducial equivalence class
paraconsistent criticality where self-measurement creates both true and false states
forward morphism continuing orbit generation through all primitive combinations
confirmation that each orbit maintains |⟨ψᵢ|ψⱼ⟩|² = 1/13 for i≠j
chaining orbit segments across all 12 primitive dimensions
progression through D-family value sets (3 values per primitive)
composition of primitive-dependent displacement pathways
validation of T-arm orbit completeness for 3×3×3 = 27 D-assignments
secondary branching for combined family interactions
continued orbit extension across temporal and processual primitives
linking D-orbits with T/P-orbits to form full classification space
self-inscription of the complete 144-element measurement structure
fusion of all primitive orbits back into unified SIC-POVM
anchoring the complete Weyl-Heisenberg covariant structure
permanent recording of the exact fiducial coordinates as fixed measurement reference
crystalline fixation of the 17,280,000-element constraint manifold
final criticality state where measurement system and measured system become equivalent
terminal closure of the d=12 SIC-POVM generation process

phase_5.compiler_frontend: Zauner symmetry enforcement mechanism ensuring orbit self-consistency
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: Weyl-Heisenberg group multiplication rules connecting displacement operators
phase_5.memory_mechanism: density matrix reconstruction from orbit measurement probabilities
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: primitive valuation sequence determining orbit generation order
phase_5.alfs_store: Crystal of Types providing the constraint lattice for valid orbits

phase_6.cycle_cost: computation of 144 displacement operations and their mutual overlaps
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: single fiducial |ψ₀⟩ in ℂ¹² with unknown orbit structure
phase_6.post_cycle_state: complete SIC-POVM with exactly determined 144 equiangular vectors
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — orbit generation preserves measurement entropy through deterministic WH covariance

  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑳 𐑼)  -- [23] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (terminal closure of the d=12 SIC-POVM generation process)
